focus: correctness: logic correctness, default/null handling, env precedence

title: Post-dispatch validation can fail a valid new iteration when an older JSONL line is malformed
detail: validateIterationOutputs() repairs only a corrupt tail, validates the newest record, then calls stampIterationLogRegion(). That helper reparses every non-empty line in the whole state log without catching JSON.parse failures. If deep-review-state.jsonl has a malformed historical line followed by a valid current iteration, repairJsonlTail() keeps the valid tail and findLastJsonlObjectRegion() throws on the older bad line, causing validateIterationOutputs() to return jsonl_parse_error instead of accepting the valid dispatch. This violates the deep-review loop contract that malformed JSONL is skipped and reconstructed from valid entries.
claim: A non-tail malformed JSONL line can make post-dispatch validation reject an otherwise valid appended iteration record.
counterevidenceSought: Checked jsonl-repair and post-dispatch tests for middle-line corruption coverage; the visible coverage repairs corrupt trailing lines but does not cover a malformed historical line followed by a valid latest iteration.
alternativeExplanation: Post-dispatch validation might intentionally be stricter than reducer replay, but the loop-level recovery contract says malformed JSONL in the loop is skipped and reconstructed from valid entries, so rejecting a valid current append on older corruption is contract drift.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if an upstream invariant guarantees state logs can only corrupt at the tail before post-dispatch validation, or if the intended contract is amended so post-dispatch validation must fail on any historical malformed line.
suggestedFix: Change findLastJsonlObjectRegion() to catch JSON.parse errors and skip malformed non-empty lines while preserving byte offsets for valid records; optionally surface a corruption advisory. Add a regression with a malformed middle line plus a valid latest iteration and assert validation stays ok and stamps logOffset/logSize/logPath.
